title: qa: Ensure consistent use of decimals instead of floats
summary: Changes functional tests to use decimal numbers instead of float numbers to fix test failures on NetBSD with newer Python versions.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves test reliability across different operating systems and Python versions, which may help ensure Bitcoin Core's testing infrastructure works consistently for developers and maintainers on various platforms.

title: ci: Switch to latest macOS and Windows images
summary: Updates the continuous integration system to use the latest macOS and Windows runner images and upgrades Xcode to version 16.2.
potential_rationale: This update likely ensures Bitcoin Core is built and tested against more current operating system versions and development tools, which may help catch compatibility issues earlier and maintain support for users on newer systems.

title: depends, NetBSD: Fix `bdb` package compilation with GCC-14
summary: Fixes compilation issues with the Berkeley DB dependency package when building on NetBSD 10 using GCC-14 compiler.
potential_rationale: This fix likely ensures Bitcoin Core can be built successfully on NetBSD systems with newer compiler versions, which may help maintain platform compatibility for users and developers working on diverse Unix-like systems.

title: build, test: Build `db_tests.cpp` regardless of `USE_BDB`
summary: Modifies the build system to always compile database tests regardless of whether Berkeley DB support is enabled, since the tests now cover both BDB and SQLite wallets.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures more comprehensive test coverage by running wallet database tests even when BDB is disabled, which may help catch SQLite wallet issues that were previously missed in certain build configurations.

title: depends: Use base system's `sha256sum` utility on FreeBSD
summary: Changes the build system to use FreeBSD's built-in sha256sum utility instead of requiring the separate shasum utility from the perl5 port.
potential_rationale: This change likely simplifies the build process on FreeBSD by removing an external dependency requirement, which may help reduce setup complexity for developers building Bitcoin Core on FreeBSD systems.

title: depends: Fix spacing issue
summary: Fixes a spacing issue in the build system where a missing space caused the AR and NM variables to be concatenated incorrectly, leading to build tool failures.
potential_rationale: This likely helps ensure reliable builds by preventing archiver and name listing tool failures that could break the Bitcoin Core compilation process on various systems.

title: depends: Override default build type for `libevent`
summary: Overrides the default build type for the libevent dependency to prevent it from using its own optimization flags that conflict with Bitcoin Core's build configuration.
potential_rationale: This may help maintain consistent optimization and debugging settings across all dependencies, which could be important for security auditing and ensuring predictable performance characteristics.

title: cmake: Do not modify `CMAKE_TRY_COMPILE_TARGET_TYPE` globally
summary: Modifies the CMake configuration to avoid globally changing the compile target type setting, which affects how CMake performs internal compilation checks.
potential_rationale: This likely improves build system reliability by preventing unintended side effects on CMake's feature detection and compilation tests, which could lead to incorrect build configurations.

title: cmake: Introduce `WITH_PYTHON` build option
summary: Introduces a WITH_PYTHON build option that makes Python availability an explicit requirement, causing builds to fail by default when the minimum Python version is not available.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve build predictability by making Python dependencies explicit rather than silently degrading functionality, which may help developers catch missing dependencies earlier in the build process.

title: cmake: Fail if `Libmultiprocess` is missing when `WITH_MULTIPROCESS=ON`
summary: Makes the build system fail explicitly when multiprocess support is enabled but the required Libmultiprocess dependency is missing.
potential_rationale: This likely prevents silent build failures or incomplete multiprocess functionality by ensuring all required dependencies are present when multiprocess features are explicitly requested by developers.

title: cmake: Copy `cov_tool_wrapper.sh.in` to the build tree
summary: Fixes a bug where the code coverage tool wrapper script wasn't accessible when running coverage analysis from different directories.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the developer experience by making code coverage testing more reliable and accessible from any working directory, which may help encourage better test coverage practices among Bitcoin Core contributors.

title: cmake: Fix `-pthread` flags presentation in summary
summary: Cleans up the CMake build summary output by removing raw generator expressions from the pthread flags display.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the developer experience by making build configuration summaries more readable and professional-looking, which may help developers better understand their build settings and catch configuration issues.

title: ci: Replace `CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS` with `APPEND_CXXFLAGS`
summary: Changes the CI system to use Bitcoin Core's custom APPEND_CXXFLAGS variable instead of CMake's standard CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS for better flag handling.
potential_rationale: This likely improves build reliability by ensuring compiler flags are applied only during compilation rather than both compilation and linking, which may help prevent subtle build issues and make the CI process more robust.

title: cmake: Generate man pages at install time
summary: Enables automatic generation of man pages during the installation process using the help2man tool.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the user experience by ensuring documentation is always up-to-date with the installed binaries, which may help users access current command-line help and reduce support burden from outdated documentation.

title: cmake: Install man pages for configured targets only
summary: Ensures that man pages are only installed for the specific Bitcoin Core components that were actually configured and built.
potential_rationale: This likely prevents installation errors and reduces disk usage by avoiding installation of documentation for components that weren't built, which may help streamline deployments and reduce confusion about available functionality.

title: cmake: Improve safety and robustness during building `crc32c` subtree
summary: Improves the safety and robustness of the custom cmake build script for the crc32c subtree by better handling upstream changes and build order dependencies.
potential_rationale: This likely helps prevent build failures and ensures that upstream changes to the crc32c library are properly tracked, which may improve the reliability of Bitcoin Core's build process and reduce maintenance overhead.

title: depends: Avoid using the `-ffile-prefix-map` compiler option
summary: Removes the `-ffile-prefix-map` compiler option from all dependency packages to fix compatibility issues with OSS-Fuzz builds.
potential_rationale: This appears to resolve build compatibility issues that may prevent Bitcoin Core from being properly tested in fuzzing environments, which could help maintain code quality and security through better automated testing coverage.

title: ci: Remove no longer needed `-Wno-error=documentation`
summary: Removes the no longer needed `-Wno-error=documentation` compiler flag from CI builds.
potential_rationale: This likely cleans up the build configuration by removing obsolete compiler flags, which may help maintain cleaner build processes and ensure documentation warnings are properly caught during development.

title: Prepare "Open Transifex translations for v29.0" release step
summary: Prepares the codebase for opening Transifex translations for the upcoming v29.0 release as part of the standard release process.
potential_rationale: This appears to be necessary for enabling community translation of the GUI for the next major release, which may help improve Bitcoin Core's accessibility to non-English speaking users worldwide.

title: cmake: Add `libbitcoinkernel` target
summary: Adds a convenience `libbitcoinkernel` target to the cmake build system and renames the bitcoinkernel component for consistency.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the developer experience by providing clearer naming and easier build targets for the kernel library, which may facilitate external projects that want to integrate with Bitcoin Core's consensus engine.

title: cmake: Add optional sources to `minisketch` library directly
summary: Improves the CMake build system by adding optional sources directly to the minisketch library and removing a workaround for a CMake bug.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the build system's maintainability and reliability by simplifying the minisketch library configuration, which may help developers compile Bitcoin Core more consistently across different environments.

title: cmake: Make implicit `libbitcoinkernel` dependencies explicit
summary: Fixes CMake build system regressions by making implicit libbitcoinkernel dependencies explicit.
potential_rationale: This appears to address build failures that could prevent developers from successfully compiling the libbitcoinkernel component, which may be critical for projects that want to integrate Bitcoin's validation logic without the full node software.

title: cmake: Exclude generated sources from translation
summary: Excludes generated source files from the translation build target to prevent build errors.
potential_rationale: This likely fixes translation workflow issues that could prevent maintainers from properly generating internationalized versions of Bitcoin Core, which may help expand Bitcoin's accessibility to non-English speaking users.

title: ci: Fix filtering out Qt-generated files from `compile_commands.json`
summary: Fixes the filtering of Qt-generated files from compile_commands.json and optimizes the clang-tidy process.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the development workflow by ensuring code analysis tools work correctly with the CMake build system, which may help developers catch bugs and maintain code quality more effectively.

title: wallet: Replace "non-0" with "non-zero" in translatable error message
summary: Changes "non-0" to "non-zero" in a translatable error message to avoid translation system warnings.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the internationalization process by preventing translation warnings that could complicate localization efforts, which may help ensure Bitcoin Core remains accessible to users in different languages.

title: qt: 29.0 translations update
summary: Updates GUI translations for the Bitcoin Core 29.0 release as part of the standard release process.
potential_rationale: This likely helps maintain Bitcoin Core's accessibility to non-English speaking users by ensuring the GUI interface is properly localized, which may contribute to broader global adoption and usability of the software.

title: ci: Do not try to install for fuzz builds
summary: Fixes CI configuration to avoid attempting installation steps for fuzz testing builds where it's not needed.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the efficiency and reliability of the continuous integration pipeline by preventing unnecessary installation steps that could cause build failures, which may help maintain faster development cycles and more stable testing infrastructure.

title: cmake: Check for `makensis` and `zip` tools before using them for optional `deploy` targets
summary: Adds checks for required tools (makensis and zip) before attempting to build deployment targets for Windows and macOS platforms.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the developer experience by providing clearer error messages when required packaging tools are missing, which may reduce build-related frustration and make it easier for contributors to set up proper build environments.

title: cmake: Add `NO_CACHE_IF_FAILED` option for checking linker flags
summary: Improves CMake configuration by adding a flag that prevents caching failed linker checks, particularly for sanitizer flags.
potential_rationale: This appears to streamline the development workflow by eliminating the need to manually clear CMake cache when dependencies are installed after initial configuration failures, which may reduce developer friction and improve build system usability.

title: Update `secp256k1` subtree to latest master
summary: Updates the secp256k1 cryptographic library subtree to the latest version from upstream.
potential_rationale: This likely brings in bug fixes, performance improvements, or security enhancements to the elliptic curve cryptography implementation that Bitcoin relies on, which may improve the overall security and efficiency of Bitcoin's cryptographic operations.

title: cmake, guix: Skip building tests in subtrees for releases
summary: Introduces a new BUILD_TEST_BINARY variable to allow building only the main test executable without building tests in dependencies like secp256k1 and univalue for release builds.
potential_rationale: This change likely aims to streamline the release build process by reducing compilation time and build complexity, which may help maintainers create releases more efficiently while still ensuring core functionality is tested.

title: doc: Adjust path in comment
summary: Adjusts a file path in a comment that was overlooked in a previous PR.
potential_rationale: This appears to be a minor documentation maintenance fix that may help prevent confusion for developers reading the code and ensure documentation accuracy after recent file reorganization.

title: build: Remove bitness suffix from Windows installer
summary: Removes the bitness suffix from Windows installer naming since 32-bit Windows support has been dropped.
potential_rationale: This likely simplifies the installation experience for Windows users and may help reduce confusion now that Bitcoin Core no longer supports 32-bit systems, potentially making the software easier to distribute and install.

title: depends: Switch from multilib to platform-specific toolchains
summary: Switches the build system from using multilib GCC toolchain to platform-specific toolchains to resolve package conflicts and improve cross-compilation capabilities.
potential_rationale: This change likely aims to make Bitcoin Core easier to build across different platforms by resolving toolchain conflicts, which may help developers and maintainers create builds more reliably and efficiently for various architectures.

title: ci: Switch to dynamic library linkage in native Windows job
summary: Changes the native Windows CI job to use dynamic library linkage instead of static linkage, reducing the vcpkg binary cache size from 2.6 GB to 430 MB.
potential_rationale: This optimization likely aims to improve CI performance and reduce infrastructure costs by dramatically shrinking cache sizes, which may help speed up automated testing and make the development process more efficient for contributors.

title: ci: Add workaround for vcpkg's `libevent` package
summary: Adds a workaround for vcpkg's libevent package to fix compatibility issues with CMake 4.0+ on Windows GitHub Actions images.
potential_rationale: This likely ensures that Bitcoin Core's continuous integration pipeline continues to function properly on Windows, which may help maintain build reliability and prevent development workflow disruptions that could slow down the project.

title: ci, windows: Do not exclude `wallet_migration.py` in command line
summary: Removes the unnecessary exclusion of wallet_migration.py test from Windows command line since the test now skips automatically.
potential_rationale: This appears to clean up test configuration by removing redundant exclusions, which may help ensure that the test suite runs more consistently and catches any future issues with wallet migration functionality on Windows.

title: doc: Amend Qt 6 dependency packages for Ubuntu
summary: Updates Ubuntu documentation to explicitly list qt6-tools-dev-tools and libgl-dev packages needed for Qt 6 on older systems like Ubuntu 22.04.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the developer onboarding experience by preventing build failures on common Ubuntu versions, which may help expand the contributor base and reduce support burden for the project.

title: msvc: Update vcpkg manifest
summary: Updates the vcpkg manifest to use newer package versions including boost 1.87.0, Qt 6.8.2, and sqlite3 3.49.1 from the March 2025 vcpkg release.
potential_rationale: This likely brings security fixes, performance improvements, and new features from updated dependencies, which may help improve Bitcoin Core's stability, security, and potentially unlock new capabilities or optimizations.

title: depends: Fix cross-compiling on macOS
summary: Fixes cross-compilation issues on macOS by specifying Objective C/C++ compilers for native Qt package and removing incompatible mv command options.
potential_rationale: This appears to restore the ability to cross-compile Bitcoin Core on macOS systems, which may help developers working on Apple platforms contribute more effectively and ensure broader platform support for the project.

title: cmake: Get rid of undocumented `BITCOIN_GENBUILD_NO_GIT` environment variable
summary: Removes an undocumented environment variable related to build information generation in the CMake build system.
potential_rationale: This likely helps simplify the build system by removing unused or unnecessary configuration options, which may improve maintainability and reduce potential sources of build inconsistencies.

title: depends: Make native package paths platform-agnostic
summary: Modifies the depends system to make native package paths work across different host platforms.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve build efficiency by allowing native packages to be reused across different compilation targets, which may reduce build times and disk usage, particularly in environments like Guix.

title: guix: Remove unused `file` package
summary: Removes the unused 'file' utility package from Guix build dependencies.
potential_rationale: This likely reduces the build dependency footprint and may speed up builds by eliminating an unnecessary package that has been obsolete since Guix builds were introduced.

title: build: Restore cross-compilation for Android
summary: Restores the ability to cross-compile Bitcoin Core for Android platforms with necessary Qt patches.
potential_rationale: This appears to expand Bitcoin Core's platform support by re-enabling Android compilation, which may help with mobile wallet development or running Bitcoin nodes on Android devices.

title: depends: Avoid `warning: "_FORTIFY_SOURCE" redefined` for `libevent`
summary: Fixes compiler warnings about redefined _FORTIFY_SOURCE when building the libevent dependency on Alpine Linux.
potential_rationale: This likely improves build reliability on Alpine Linux by eliminating compiler warnings that could potentially mask real issues or cause build failures in stricter compilation environments.

title: build: Fix `macdeployqtplus` after switching to Qt 6
summary: Fixes the macdeployqtplus script to work properly with Qt 6 on macOS by handling differences in library path references compared to Qt 5.
potential_rationale: This likely helps maintain the ability to build Bitcoin Core GUI applications on macOS as the project transitions to newer Qt versions, which may be important for keeping the software accessible to Mac users and maintaining cross-platform compatibility.

title: doc: Add note for building on macOS (Intel) with CMake ≥ 4.0
summary: Adds documentation notes for building Bitcoin Core on Intel-based macOS systems when using CMake version 4.0 or later to address warning suppression issues.
potential_rationale: This appears to help developers avoid build warnings that could potentially mask real issues, and may improve the overall developer experience when building Bitcoin Core on macOS Intel systems with newer CMake versions.

title: ci: Temporarily disable `WalletMigration` benchmark
summary: Temporarily disables the WalletMigration benchmark test that is currently failing in the continuous integration system.
potential_rationale: This likely helps maintain CI stability by preventing build failures, which may be important for keeping the development workflow smooth while the underlying benchmark issue gets resolved properly.

title: ci: Drop no longer necessary `-Wno-error=array-bounds`
summary: Removes the unnecessary `-Wno-error=array-bounds` compiler flag from the Windows cross-compilation CI job since the warnings it was suppressing no longer appear.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve code quality by allowing the compiler to catch potential array bounds issues that could lead to security vulnerabilities or crashes, and may help maintain cleaner build configurations.

title: test: Treat executable paths in tests consistently
summary: Makes the handling of executable paths in tests more consistent, particularly for multi-config CMake generators that use per-configuration subdirectories.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the reliability of the test suite across different build configurations, which may be important for ensuring Bitcoin Core works correctly regardless of how it's built and tested by developers.

title: test: Increase stack size for "Debug" builds with MSVC
summary: Increases stack size for Debug builds with MSVC compiler to handle deep recursion in miniscript test functions.
potential_rationale: This likely helps ensure Bitcoin's miniscript testing can run properly on Windows development environments, which may be important for maintaining cross-platform compatibility and allowing developers on Windows to contribute effectively.

title: [29.x] qt: 29.1 translations update
summary: Updates translations for Bitcoin Core version 29.1 by fetching recent changes from Transifex translation platform.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve Bitcoin Core's accessibility to non-English speaking users, which may help with global adoption and usability by providing up-to-date localized interfaces.

title: cmake: Respect user-provided configuration-specific flags
summary: Fixes CMake build system to properly respect user-provided compiler flags for specific build configurations like Release mode.
potential_rationale: This likely helps developers and packagers who need specific compiler optimizations or flags, which may be important for performance tuning or meeting distribution requirements for Bitcoin Core builds.

title: depends: Fix cross-compiling `qt` package from macOS to Windows
summary: Fixes cross-compilation issues when building the Qt package from macOS to Windows by disabling unnecessary Qt behavior.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the cross-platform build process, which may help developers create Windows binaries from macOS systems more reliably, potentially streamlining release processes and development workflows.

title: subprocess: Backport upstream changes
summary: Backports upstream improvements to the subprocess utility header that were previously developed for Bitcoin Core and contributed back to the original project.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the reliability and functionality of Bitcoin Core's subprocess handling utilities, which may help with various internal processes and external tool integration that Bitcoin Core relies on.

title: cmake: Check user-defined `APPEND_*FLAGS` variables early
summary: Fixes the build system to properly handle user-defined compiler flags during early build checks. This ensures that custom flags are consistently applied throughout the entire build process.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the reliability of Bitcoin Core builds across different environments and configurations, which may help reduce build failures and support issues that could affect developer productivity and software distribution.

title: Modernize use of UTF-8 in Windows code
summary: Updates Windows code to use modern UTF-8 handling approaches and removes deprecated code that will be removed in C++26. This modernizes how Bitcoin Core handles Unicode text on Windows systems.
potential_rationale: This change appears to future-proof Bitcoin Core's Windows compatibility and may improve handling of non-English characters in file paths and user interfaces, potentially making the software more accessible to international users.

title: util: Remove `fsbridge::get_filesystem_error_message()`
summary: Removes the `fsbridge::get_filesystem_error_message()` function which is no longer needed after migrating to std::filesystem and fixes UTF-8 path display issues on Windows.
potential_rationale: This cleanup likely simplifies the codebase and may improve error message clarity for Windows users, potentially making troubleshooting filesystem-related issues easier for both users and developers.

title: cmake: Add application manifests when cross-compiling for Windows
summary: Adds Windows application manifests during cross-compilation, which enables enhanced security settings and better control over Windows version compatibility.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves Bitcoin Core's security posture on Windows and may help ensure proper compatibility across different Windows versions, potentially reducing runtime issues and improving the overall user experience on Windows systems.

title: test: Introduce `SUPPRESS_ABORT_MESSAGE` environment variable
summary: Introduces an environment variable to suppress abort message boxes when running tests on Windows with debug runtime libraries. This prevents test timeouts caused by modal dialogs in CI environments.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the reliability of automated testing on Windows, which may help maintain code quality and reduce false positives in continuous integration, ultimately supporting more stable Bitcoin Core development processes.

title: cmake: Allow `WITH_DBUS` on all Unix-like systems
summary: Extends D-Bus support configuration option to all Unix-like systems instead of restricting it to Linux only. This fixes a regression introduced during the migration from Autotools to CMake build system.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves build system flexibility and may help developers on various Unix-like platforms configure Bitcoin Core with D-Bus support if needed, though the practical impact appears limited since macOS uses different notification systems.

title: doc: Fix typo
summary: Fixes a minor typo in documentation by replacing double dots (..) with proper ellipsis (…) formatting. The issue was identified by a translator working on Transifex.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the professional appearance of Bitcoin Core's user interface and may help ensure consistent formatting across different language translations.

title: cmake: Restrict MSVC-specific workaround to affected versions
summary: Removes a compiler workaround that was only needed for older MSVC versions, allowing the fuzz/utxo_snapshot.cpp file to compile normally with MSVC 17.14 and later. The workaround is now restricted to only the affected older compiler versions.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves code maintainability and may help ensure that fuzzing tests for UTXO snapshots can run properly on Windows systems with updated compilers, potentially strengthening Bitcoin Core's testing coverage.

title: Set minimum supported Windows version to 1903 (May 2019 Update)
summary: Updates the minimum supported Windows version to 1903 (May 2019 Update) and adds verification in the symbol-check.py script to ensure application manifests specify supported OS versions. This change enables UTF-8 code page support required for another PR.
potential_rationale: This change likely modernizes Bitcoin Core's Windows compatibility and may help ensure proper Unicode text handling on Windows systems, which could be important for international users and wallet functionality involving non-ASCII characters.

title: cmake: Add missed `SSE41_CXXFLAGS`

potential_rationale: This change likely ensures that SSE4.1 optimizations are properly enabled during compilation, which may help improve Bitcoin Core's performance on systems that support these CPU instructions, particularly for cryptographic operations.

title: cmake: Remove `ENABLE_{SSE41,AVX2,X86_SHANI,ARM_SHANI}` from `bitcoin-build-config.h`
summary: Removes redundant global definitions of CPU feature flags from the build configuration header since they're already defined locally where needed.

title: refactor: Remove workaround for resolved MSVC bug

title: miniscript, refactor: Make `operator""_mst` `consteval` (re-take)

title: subprocess: Backport upstream changes
summary: Updates the embedded subprocess library by backporting upstream bug fixes and improvements from the original cpp-subprocess project.

title: subprocess: Let shell parse command on non-Windows systems

title: test: Fix `system_tests/run_command` test
summary: Fixes a test that checks command execution by ensuring it validates the exact exit code (1) and properly handles error message parsing by removing process invocation strings from exception messages.
potential_rationale: This fix likely ensures the test suite accurately validates error handling behavior, which may help catch regressions in command-line interface functionality and improve overall code reliability.

title: windows: Use predefined `RC_INVOKED` macro instead of custom one

title: build: Add resource file and manifest to `bitcoin.exe`

title: cmake: Replace deprecated `qt6_add_translation` with `qt6_add_lrelease`

title: doc: Remove build instruction for running `clang-tidy`
summary: Removes outdated build instructions for running clang-tidy since the CMake compilation database allows running it without building the code first.

title: build: Find Boost in config mode
summary: Updates the build system to find Boost libraries using config mode instead of the deprecated FindBoost module.
potential_rationale: This appears to be necessary for compatibility with newer CMake versions and may help ensure Bitcoin Core continues building correctly as build tools evolve.

title: refactor: Drop unused `#include <boost/operators.hpp>`
summary: Removes an unused include of boost/operators.hpp header from the mini_miner.cpp file.

title: cmake: Fix `FindQt` module

title: depends: Override host compilers for FreeBSD and OpenBSD

title: doc: Update Qt 6 packages on FreeBSD

title: guix: Build for macOS using Clang only

title: cmake: Create subdirectories in build tree in advance

title: Fix build on macOS when `qt@6` is installed

title: cmake: Explicitly specify `Boost_ROOT` for Homebrew's package

title: Update `minisketch` subtree

title: doc: Add workaround for vcpkg issue with paths with embedded spaces
summary: Adds documentation workaround for a vcpkg issue that occurs when building on Windows systems with file paths containing embedded spaces.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the developer experience on Windows by preventing build failures in common scenarios where users have spaces in their directory names, which may help lower barriers to contribution and testing on Windows platforms.

title: cmake: Use newer signature of `qt6_add_lrelease` when available
summary: Updates the CMake build system to use a newer signature of the qt6_add_lrelease function when it's available, fixing a compatibility issue.
potential_rationale: This likely helps ensure Bitcoin Core can build successfully with newer versions of Qt6, which may be important for maintaining compatibility with modern development environments and ensuring the GUI remains buildable across different Qt versions.

title: depends: Force `CMAKE_EXPORT_NO_PACKAGE_REGISTRY=TRUE`
summary: Forces CMake to disable package registry population by setting CMAKE_EXPORT_NO_PACKAGE_REGISTRY=TRUE globally to prevent build artifacts from being written outside the build tree.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve build isolation and reproducibility by preventing CMake from writing to user package registries outside the build directory, which may help ensure cleaner builds and reduce potential conflicts in development environments.

title: build: Explicitly set Qt's `AUTO{MOC,RCC,UIC}` property per target
summary: Explicitly sets Qt's AUTOMOC, AUTORCC, and AUTOUIC properties per target rather than relying on default behavior for handling Qt's code generation tools.
potential_rationale: This likely provides more explicit control over Qt's automatic code generation processes, which may help ensure consistent behavior across different CMake versions and improve build reliability for the GUI components.

title: [POC] ci: Skip compilation when running static code analysis
summary: Proof of concept that skips compilation when running static code analysis tools like clang-tidy by using a compilation database instead.
potential_rationale: This may significantly speed up static analysis in continuous integration by avoiding the need to actually compile the code when only running analysis tools, potentially reducing CI runtime and resource usage while still maintaining code quality checks.

title: cmake: Drop no longer necessary "cmakeMinimumRequired" object
summary: Removes a no longer necessary cmakeMinimumRequired object from the build system since the minimum CMake version is already specified as 3.22.
potential_rationale: This appears to be a cleanup that removes redundant code now that Bitcoin Core has established a minimum CMake version requirement, likely simplifying the build configuration and reducing maintenance overhead.

title: kernel: create monolithic kernel static library
summary: Creates a monolithic static library for the Bitcoin kernel that includes all dependency object files in a single library file. This simplifies distribution by eliminating the need for separate dependency libraries when using libbitcoinkernel.a.
potential_rationale: This change likely makes it easier for external projects to integrate Bitcoin's kernel library by reducing dependency management complexity, which may encourage broader adoption of Bitcoin Core's validation and consensus logic in other applications.

title: cmake: Proactively avoid use of `SECP256K1_DISABLE_SHARED`
summary: Removes usage of the SECP256K1_DISABLE_SHARED CMake variable that has been deprecated upstream. This proactively prevents build breakage when the secp256k1 library is next updated.
potential_rationale: This appears to be maintenance work that ensures Bitcoin Core's build system remains compatible with upstream dependencies, likely preventing future compilation failures and keeping the codebase aligned with modern cryptographic library standards.

title: refactor: Use immediate lambda to work around GCC bug 117966
summary: Implements a workaround using immediate lambda functions to fix a compilation bug in GCC versions 13 and 14 when using the debug flag. This resolves build failures on affected compiler versions.
potential_rationale: This fix likely ensures Bitcoin Core can be compiled and debugged on a wider range of development environments, which may improve developer productivity and make the codebase more accessible to contributors using newer GCC versions.

title: cmake: Switch to generated `ts_files.cmake` file
summary: Restructures Qt translation file handling by moving it to a dedicated locale directory and switching from file globbing to an explicit list generated by maintainer tools. This improves the reliability of translation file management in the CMake build system.
potential_rationale: This change likely makes the build process more deterministic and maintainable for internationalization, which may improve Bitcoin Core's accessibility to non-English speaking users by ensuring more reliable translation integration.

title: Release: Prepare "Open Transifex translations for v30.0" step
summary: Prepares the necessary infrastructure to open Transifex translations for the upcoming v30.0 release. This follows the established release process for managing internationalization workflow.
potential_rationale: This appears to be part of the standard release preparation process that ensures Bitcoin Core v30.0 can be properly translated into multiple languages, likely improving global accessibility and user adoption of the new version.

title: cmake: Do not require Python to build GUI
summary: This PR modifies the CMake build system to remove the Python dependency when building the Bitcoin Core GUI.
potential_rationale: This change likely simplifies the build process for developers who only want to build the GUI components, potentially reducing barriers to contribution and making the build system more modular by removing unnecessary dependencies.

title: Avoid file overwriting in fallback `AllocateFileRange` implementation
summary: This PR fixes a bug in the fallback AllocateFileRange implementation that was overwriting file content, causing issues during blockchain reindexing on certain operating systems.
potential_rationale: This fix appears to be critical for ensuring Bitcoin Core works correctly on OpenBSD, NetBSD, OmniOS, and OpenIndiana systems, likely preventing data corruption during reindex operations which could compromise node reliability on these platforms.

title: ci: Update `actions/checkout` version
summary: This PR updates the GitHub Actions checkout action to version 5.0.0 in the continuous integration system.
potential_rationale: Updating CI dependencies may help ensure the automated testing infrastructure remains secure and functional, likely providing access to newer features and bug fixes that could improve the development workflow.

title: Release: Prepare "Translation string freeze" step
summary: This PR prepares for the translation string freeze step in the Bitcoin Core release process by updating translatable strings.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures that new user-facing text added in recent PRs can be properly translated by the community, potentially improving accessibility for non-English speaking Bitcoin users worldwide.

title: cmake: Collect headers for extracting translatable strings
summary: This PR modifies the CMake build system to include header files when extracting translatable strings for the translation process.
potential_rationale: This improvement likely ensures that translatable strings located in header files are not missed during the extraction process, potentially providing more complete translations for the user interface and improving the international user experience.

title: Fix compatibility with `-debuglogfile` command-line option
summary: Fixes hardcoded 'debug.log' filename to properly support custom debug log filenames specified via the `-debuglogfile` command-line option.
potential_rationale: This likely improves operational flexibility for node operators who may want to customize log file locations or naming schemes, which could be important for deployment automation or compliance with organizational logging standards.

title: Release: 30.0 translations update
summary: Updates translations for the 30.0 release as part of the standard release process workflow.
potential_rationale: Translation updates may help broaden Bitcoin Core's accessibility to non-English speaking users, potentially supporting wider global adoption and usability of the reference implementation.

title: clang-tidy: Disable `UndefinedBinaryOperatorResult` check in `src/ipc`
summary: Disables a clang-tidy check that produces false positive warnings in IPC-related source files generated by the mpgen tool.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the development experience by reducing noise from static analysis tools, which may help developers focus on legitimate code quality issues and maintain cleaner build processes.

title: Fix benchmark CSV output
summary: Replaces comma with semicolon in benchmark naming to fix broken CSV output format in bench_bitcoin.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the reliability of performance testing workflows, which may be important for developers and researchers analyzing Bitcoin Core's performance characteristics over time.

title: cmake: Fix regression in `secp256k1.cmake`
summary: Fixes a CMake regression in secp256k1 configuration that violated CMake's function scope restrictions for enable_language() calls.
potential_rationale: This appears to restore proper CMake build functionality, which may be critical for developers using CMake-based build workflows and could prevent build failures on certain platforms or configurations.

title: cmake: Install `bitcoin` manpage
summary: This PR adds the installation of the bitcoin manpage to the CMake build system. It appears to be a follow-up amendment to a previous PR that may have missed including the manpage installation.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the user experience for those building Bitcoin from source by ensuring documentation is properly installed with the binary, which may help with adoption and ease of use.

title: msvc: Update vcpkg manifest
summary: This PR updates the vcpkg manifest for Windows builds, upgrading several dependencies including Boost (1.87.0 to 1.88.0), Qt components (6.8.2 to 6.9.1), and SQLite (3.49.1 to 3.50.4).
potential_rationale: Updating dependencies likely brings security fixes, performance improvements, and bug fixes from upstream projects, which may enhance Bitcoin's stability and security on Windows platforms.

title: ci: Update Clang in "tidy" job
summary: This PR updates the CI system to use IWYU 0.25 with Clang 21 and fixes new compiler warnings, including suppressing a false positive in the interpreter code.
potential_rationale: Keeping CI tools updated likely helps maintain code quality and catches potential issues earlier in development, while fixing compiler warnings may prevent future bugs and improve code maintainability.

title: doc: Add `INSTALL.md` to Linux release tarballs
summary: This PR adds installation instructions (INSTALL.md) to Linux release tarballs to provide setup guidance for popular desktop distributions.
potential_rationale: Including installation instructions in release packages likely reduces barriers to adoption by making it easier for users to properly install Bitcoin on their Linux systems without needing to search for documentation elsewhere.

title: Release: 30.0rc2 translations update
summary: This PR updates Spanish and Czech translations for the 30.0rc2 release candidate, addressing specific review comments from the translation process.
potential_rationale: Improving translations likely makes Bitcoin more accessible to non-English speaking users, which may help with global adoption and usability of the software.

title: depends: Update URL for `qrencode` package source tarball
summary: Updates the download URL for the qrencode package source tarball from fukuchi.org to GitHub releases page due to the original source being unavailable.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures the Bitcoin Core build system can continue to reliably download dependencies needed for QR code functionality, which may be important for wallet address sharing and payment request features.

title: Release: 30.0rc3 translations update
summary: Updates Polish translation strings for the 30.0rc3 release candidate while skipping other language updates to focus review effort.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve accessibility for Polish-speaking Bitcoin users by providing more accurate translations, which may help expand Bitcoin adoption in Polish-speaking regions.

title: [28.x] ci: Fix Qt 5.15 URL
summary: Fixes the Qt 5.15 download URL in the continuous integration system for the 28.x branch.
potential_rationale: This likely ensures that automated builds on the 28.x branch can continue to function properly by accessing the correct Qt library version, which may be critical for maintaining GUI functionality in older releases.

title: guix: Use UCRT runtime for Windows release binaries
summary: Switches Windows release binaries from MSVCRT to UCRT runtime and removes related workarounds while updating documentation.
potential_rationale: This change likely modernizes Windows compatibility and may improve performance or security by using a more current runtime library, potentially making Bitcoin Core more reliable on newer Windows systems.

title: ci, iwyu: Treat warnings as errors for `src/init` and `src/policy`
summary: Extends the Include What You Use (IWYU) tool to treat warnings as errors in the src/init and src/policy directories to improve code quality.
potential_rationale: This appears to enforce stricter code hygiene standards which may help prevent compilation issues and improve maintainability of critical Bitcoin Core components related to initialization and policy validation.

title: ci: Add Windows + UCRT jobs for cross-compiling and native testing
summary: Adds new CI jobs for Windows builds using the modern UCRT runtime instead of the older MSVCRT runtime. This includes both cross-compilation and native testing jobs.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves Windows compatibility and modernizes the build infrastructure, which may help ensure Bitcoin Core works reliably on newer Windows systems and appears to be preparing for eventual removal of legacy runtime support.

title: cmake: Move IPC tests to `ipc/test`
summary: Reorganizes IPC (Inter-Process Communication) tests in the CMake build system by moving them to a dedicated `ipc/test` directory. Also removes an unused clang-tidy configuration file.
potential_rationale: This organizational change likely improves code maintainability and may make it easier for developers to locate and work with IPC-related tests, which appears to support Bitcoin Core's modular architecture efforts.

title: ci, iwyu: Fix warnings in `src/kernel` and treat them as errors
summary: Updates include-what-you-use (IWYU) checks to fix warnings in the `src/kernel` directory and configures the system to treat these warnings as errors.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves code quality and compilation reliability by ensuring proper header dependencies in the kernel module, which may help maintain clean separation between Bitcoin Core's components and reduce build issues.

title: clang-tidy: Remove no longer needed NOLINT
summary: Removes unnecessary NOLINT comments from the codebase that were previously needed to suppress clang-tidy warnings but are no longer required.
potential_rationale: This cleanup likely reduces code noise and improves maintainability by removing obsolete linter suppressions, which may help developers focus on legitimate code quality issues without being distracted by outdated annotations.

title: ci: Add IWYU job
summary: Creates a separate CI job specifically for include-what-you-use (IWYU) checks, moving them out of the existing tidy job to provide faster feedback to developers.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the development workflow by providing quicker feedback on header dependency issues, which may help developers catch and fix include problems earlier in the development process without waiting for longer-running CI jobs to complete.

title: ci: Extend tidy job to cover kernel code
summary: Extends the continuous integration tidy job to include kernel code in automated code quality checks.
potential_rationale: This likely helps maintain code quality standards across Bitcoin's kernel components, which may be critical for ensuring the reliability and maintainability of core consensus and validation logic.

title: scripted-diff: Remove obsolete comment
summary: Removes outdated comments that became obsolete after previous changes to the codebase.
potential_rationale: This appears to help keep the codebase documentation current and accurate, which may reduce developer confusion and improve code maintainability.

title: doc: Correct `pkgin` command usage on NetBSD
summary: Fixes documentation for the pkgin package manager command on NetBSD by adding the required 'install' subcommand.
potential_rationale: This likely helps NetBSD users correctly install Bitcoin dependencies, potentially expanding the platform compatibility and user base for Bitcoin Core.

title: cmake: Switch to `minisketch` upstream build system
summary: Changes the build system to use minisketch's native build system instead of a custom integration.
potential_rationale: This may help maintain better compatibility with upstream minisketch updates and could reduce maintenance burden by leveraging the library's official build configuration.

title: test, refactor: Fix `-Warray-bounds` warning
summary: Fixes a compiler warning about array bounds that occurs when using GCC 14 for Windows cross-compilation.
potential_rationale: This likely helps ensure Bitcoin Core can be built cleanly with newer compiler versions, which may be important for maintaining build compatibility and catching potential memory safety issues.

title: doc: Add `x86_64-w64-mingw32ucrt` triplet to `depends/README.md`
summary: Updates documentation to include the new x86_64-w64-mingw32ucrt build target for Windows cross-compilation. This is part of migrating to the modern UCRT runtime for Windows builds.
potential_rationale: This likely helps modernize Bitcoin Core's Windows builds by moving to a more current runtime environment, which may improve compatibility with newer Windows systems and development tools.

title: build: Bump VS minimum supported version to 18.3
summary: Increases the minimum required Visual Studio version to 18.3 for Windows builds. This change addresses bug fixes and resolves a specific vcpkg issue.
potential_rationale: This appears to ensure more reliable Windows builds by requiring a Visual Studio version with important bug fixes, which may reduce build failures and improve development experience for Windows contributors.

title: cmake: Specify Windows plugin path in `test_bitcoin-qt` property
summary: Configures CMake to automatically set the Qt plugin path for Windows test builds. This removes the need for developers to manually set the QT_PLUGIN_PATH environment variable.
potential_rationale: This likely streamlines the testing process on Windows by eliminating manual configuration steps, which may encourage more thorough testing and reduce setup friction for developers working on GUI components.

title: doc: Update NetBSD Build Guide
summary: Updates NetBSD build documentation to use Python 3.13 packages instead of Python 3.10 packages. This reflects the current package availability on NetBSD 10.1.
potential_rationale: This appears to ensure that NetBSD users can successfully build Bitcoin Core by referencing packages that are actually available in the current NetBSD package repository, which may help maintain platform compatibility.

title: depends: Add patch for Windows11Style plugin
summary: Adds a patch for the Windows11Style Qt plugin to fix GUI styling issues. This addresses a specific visual problem in the Bitcoin Core interface on Windows.
potential_rationale: This likely improves the user experience on Windows by fixing GUI rendering problems, which may help Bitcoin Core appear more professional and native on Windows 11 systems.

title: depends: Update Qt download link
summary: Updates the Qt download link in the build system from an unreliable source to a GitHub mirror. This addresses issues with the previous download location being unavailable.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves build reliability for developers and CI systems by ensuring the Qt dependency can be consistently downloaded, which may help reduce build failures and improve the development experience.

title: qa: Remove no longer needed `feature_dirsymlinks.py`
summary: Removes an obsolete functional test file that was no longer needed after previous cleanup of unused directory creation workarounds. This follows up on earlier refactoring work.
potential_rationale: This cleanup likely reduces maintenance burden by removing dead code and may help keep the test suite focused on relevant functionality, potentially improving test execution time and reducing confusion for developers.

title: test: Remove `system_tests/run_command` runtime dependencies
summary: Removes runtime dependencies on system utilities like 'cat', 'echo', 'false', and 'sh' from system tests. This reduces platform-specific code paths and external dependencies.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves build portability across different systems and package managers, and may help reduce the complexity of packaging Bitcoin Core for various distributions by eliminating external tool dependencies.

title: cmake: Set `WITH_ZMQ` to `ON` in Windows presets
summary: Enables the ZMQ (ZeroMQ) configuration option by default in Windows CMake build presets. The ZeroMQ feature was already enabled in the package configuration but not in the build preset.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures consistency between package configuration and build settings on Windows, and may help Windows users access ZMQ functionality for receiving Bitcoin Core notifications without additional configuration steps.

title: cmake: Make `BUILD_KERNEL_TEST` depend on `BUILD_KERNEL_LIB`
summary: Fixes CMake configuration to make the kernel test build option properly depend on the kernel library build option. This corrects misleading configuration output that showed kernel tests as enabled when the underlying library was disabled.
potential_rationale: This change likely prevents build errors and confusion by ensuring logical dependencies are properly enforced in the build system, which may help developers avoid invalid build configurations when working with experimental kernel components.

title: cmake: Check dependencies after build option interaction
summary: Reorganizes the CMake configuration to check dependencies only after build options are finalized, avoiding unnecessary dependency checks. This appears to optimize the build configuration process by eliminating redundant work.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves build performance and reduces complexity by ensuring dependency discovery only occurs for features that are actually enabled, which may help streamline the development workflow and reduce build times.

title: depends, doc: Add `tcl` as build dependency for `sqlite` package
summary: Adds `tcl` as a build dependency for the `sqlite` package in the depends system and updates documentation. This addresses build failures when neither `jimsh` nor `tclsh` are available on the system.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures more reliable builds across different environments by explicitly declaring a required dependency, which may help prevent build failures on systems that don't have the necessary tools pre-installed.

title: depends: Propagate native C compiler to `sqlite` package
summary: Ensures the native C compiler is properly propagated to the `sqlite` package build process and removes the `tcl` package from the Guix manifest. This provides an alternative solution to build issues with sqlite.
potential_rationale: This approach likely provides a more elegant solution to sqlite build issues by leveraging existing compiler tooling rather than adding new dependencies, which may help maintain build system simplicity while ensuring compatibility.

title: cmake: Add fail pattern to `try_append_cxx_flags`
summary: Adds a fail pattern to the `try_append_cxx_flags` function in CMake to handle cases where build environments provide flags that interfere with compiler flag detection. This addresses issues in environments like OSS-Fuzz.
potential_rationale: This change likely ensures more reliable compiler flag detection in specialized build environments, which may help maintain code quality standards and prevent compilation issues in automated testing and fuzzing infrastructures.

title: net: Fix `-Wmissing-braces`
summary: Fixes `-Wmissing-braces` warnings that occur on some non-POSIX platforms when using certain network-related macros. This addresses compiler warnings specifically seen on platforms like OpenIndiana/illumos.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves Bitcoin Core's portability across different operating systems by resolving platform-specific compiler warnings, which may help ensure the software can be built and run reliably on a broader range of systems.

title: cmake: Add missed `Boost::headers`
summary: Adds missing Boost::headers dependency to the CMake build system to fix compilation failures on Fedora 43.
potential_rationale: This likely ensures that Bitcoin Core can be reliably built on newer Linux distributions, which may help maintain broader compatibility and reduce build issues for developers and packagers.

title: build: Update minimum required Boost version
summary: Updates the minimum required Boost version from 1.73.0 to 1.74.0 due to build incompatibilities introduced by recent changes.
potential_rationale: This appears to maintain build system stability by requiring a Boost version that's compatible with recent code changes, likely preventing compilation errors while still supporting all major distributions.

title: cmake: Fix debugging info
summary: Fixes debugging information issues in the CMake build by reverting a previous change that caused multiple unintended problems.
potential_rationale: This may help developers debug Bitcoin Core more effectively by ensuring proper debugging symbols are generated, which could facilitate faster development and troubleshooting of issues.

title: iwyu: Add `pragma: always_keep` to `bitcoin-build-config.h`
summary: Adds an 'always_keep' pragma to the bitcoin-build-config.h header to streamline include-what-you-use (IWYU) tool usage.
potential_rationale: This likely improves code maintenance efficiency by reducing repetitive pragma statements throughout the codebase, which may help developers keep headers organized and dependencies explicit.

title: build: Prevent system header fallback and include path pollution
summary: Prevents system header fallback and include path pollution in the depends build subsystem to avoid masked missing dependencies.
potential_rationale: This appears to make the build system more robust by ensuring dependencies are explicitly declared rather than accidentally satisfied, which may prevent future build breakages when dependencies change.

title: qa: Require `--exclude` for each excluded test
summary: This PR modifies the test runner to allow the `--exclude` argument to be split across multiple lines with optional comments for each excluded test. This makes it easier to manage and document long lists of excluded tests in CI scripts.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves the maintainability of CI scripts by allowing developers to document why specific tests are excluded and organize exclusions more clearly. This may help reduce confusion when managing complex test suites and could make it easier to track which tests need attention.

title: doc: Update OpenBSD Build Guide
summary: This PR updates the OpenBSD build documentation to reflect that OpenBSD 7.8 now ships with LLVM/Clang 19.1.7. This version meets Bitcoin Core's recently updated minimum compiler requirements.
potential_rationale: This documentation update likely helps OpenBSD users understand that they can now build Bitcoin Core with the system-provided compiler without needing to install additional toolchain components. This may reduce build complexity and improve the user experience for developers on OpenBSD systems.
